title: A foundation model for the Earth system
methods: in-memory replay buffer for long-lead-time training
latitude-weighted RMSE and anomaly correlation coefficient
thresholded RMSE for extreme event evaluation
data_sources: CAMS global atmospheric composition forecasts and analysis

data_scope.pretraining_volume: more than 1 million hours of diverse geophysical data
data_scope.cams_training_period: October 2017 to May 2022 (fine-tuning); May to November 2022 (test)
data_scope.data_categories: analysis, reanalysis, forecast, reforecast, climate simulation

core_conclusion: Aurora is a 1.3-billion-parameter foundation model for the Earth system, pretrained on more than one million hours of diverse geophysical data. It outperforms specialized operational forecasting systems for air quality (74% of targets vs CAMS), ocean waves (86% of variables vs HRES-WAM), tropical cyclone tracks (100% of agencies worldwide), and high-resolution weather (92% of targets vs IFS HRES) — all at orders of magnitude lower computational cost — marking the first time an AI model surpasses full operational tropical cyclone forecasts up to 5 days.
why_it_matters: First cross-domain Earth system foundation model unifying four forecasting domains in a single pretrained backbone; surpasses operational systems across all four domains; ~100,000x speedup over CAMS for air quality; pretrain-once-fine-tune-cheaply paradigm (4-8 weeks vs years); open weights and code democratize access.
why_nature: Landmark technical advance with immediate societal impact on natural disaster mitigation; universal paradigm shift reframing geophysical modeling; rigorous multi-domain benchmarking against four independent operational systems; open weights and reproducible benchmarks amplify long-term scientific impact.
added_value_to_my_research: Fine-tune Aurora's air quality representations on urban sensor networks -> dynamic pollution-adjusted 2SFCA accessibility metric
Aurora's Perceiver heterogeneous encoder as template for unified urban spatiotemporal data fusion (mobile phone, social media, POIs)
Replace Aurora's heuristic vortex tracker with explainable spatial GNN -> parallels physics-informed graph learning for disease outbreak tracking
Couple Aurora wave forecasts to coastal flood exposure -> flood-adjusted healthcare accessibility under disaster scenarios
Aurora latent representations as feature extractor for coupled climate-mode model -> seasonal forcing for urban health analytics

related.title: Advancing operational global aerosol forecasting with machine learning
related.relation: complementary ML approach for global aerosol/air quality forecasting, same operational context

related.title: Multi-task learning for medical foundation models
related.relation: multi-task pretraining on heterogeneous data as a foundation model paradigm, analogous to Aurora's cross-domain approach

related.title: Neural general circulation models for weather and climate
related.relation: neural GCM combining learned dynamics with physical priors — same weather/climate AI domain

related.title: Learning the coupled dynamics of global climate modes
related.relation: data-driven modeling of coupled climate teleconnections — natural extension of Aurora to seasonal timescales

related.title: Atmospheric wind energization of ocean weather
related.relation: atmosphere-ocean coupling dynamics directly relevant to Aurora's wave forecasting domain

figures.caption: Aurora architecture: 1.3B-parameter model with 3D Perceiver encoder/decoder and 3D Swin Transformer backbone; pretraining on heterogeneous datasets and fine-tuning for four operational tasks
Aurora vs CAMS for air quality — TC NO2 predictions and scorecard showing Aurora matches or outperforms CAMS on 74% of all targets and 89% at 3-day lead
Aurora vs HRES-WAM for ocean waves — Typhoon Nanmadol significant wave height case study; outperforms on 86% of wave variables and 91% at 3-day lead
Aurora vs seven operational agencies for tropical cyclone tracks — track prediction MAE scorecard; Typhoon Doksuri landfall prediction case study; 20-24% improvement across basins
Aurora at 0.1° vs IFS HRES — outperforms on 92% of targets; only AI model to accurately predict Storm Ciarán maximum wind-speed spike; wind speed RMSE at 13,000+ stations
